sync.c(3) Library Functions Manual sync.c(3)

NAME

sync.c - 🔒 Synchronization primitive debugging with dynamic state inspection

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct sync_buffer_t
struct debug_state_request_t

Macros


#define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536

Enumerations


enum debug_request_type_t { DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE, DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE, DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT }

Functions


void debug_sync_print_state (void)
void debug_sync_final_cleanup (void)
Final cleanup of all debug allocations at shutdown. void debug_sync_print_state_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)
Schedule delayed debug state printing on debug thread. void debug_sync_print_backtrace_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)
Schedule delayed backtrace printing on debug thread. void debug_sync_set_memory_report_interval (uint64_t interval_ns)
Set periodic memory report interval. void debug_sync_set_main_thread_id (void)
int debug_sync_init (void)
uint64_t debug_sync_get_main_thread_id (void)
bool debug_sync_is_cleanup_in_progress (void)
int debug_sync_start_thread (void)
void debug_sync_destroy (void)
void debug_sync_cleanup_thread (void)
void debug_sync_trigger_print (void)
void debug_sync_get_stats (uint64_t *total_acquired, uint64_t *total_released, uint32_t *currently_held)
int debug_sync_mutex_lock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_mutex_trylock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_mutex_unlock (mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_rdlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_rdunlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_wrlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_rwlock_wrunlock (rwlock_t *lock, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_wait (cond_t *cond, mutex_t *mutex, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_timedwait (cond_t *cond, mutex_t *mutex, uint64_t timeout_ns, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_signal (cond_t *cond, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
int debug_sync_cond_broadcast (cond_t *cond, const char *file_name, int line_number, const char *function_name)
bool debug_sync_is_initialized (void)

Detailed Description

🔒 Synchronization primitive debugging with dynamic state inspection

Date

February 2026

Clean architecture: queries named.c registry for current state. No internal collection overhead - inspects lock structs directly.

Definition in file sync.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536

Referenced by debug_sync_print_state().

Enumerator

DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE
DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE
DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT

Definition at line 426 of file sync.c.

426              {
427   DEBUG_REQUEST_STATE,         // Print sync state
428   DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE,     // Print backtrace
429   DEBUG_REQUEST_MEMORY_REPORT, // Print memory report
430 } debug_request_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




void
  debug_sync_cleanup_thread (void )


Definition at line 665 of file sync.c.


665                                      {
666   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Starting cleanup");
667 
668   // Only join if thread was actually created
669   if (!g_debug_state_request.initialized) {
670     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread not initialized, returning");
671     return;
672   }
673 
674   // Set cleanup flag to prevent deadlock checks from accessing freed memory
675   // Do this after checking initialization so we don't set it unnecessarily
676   atomic_store_bool(&g_cleanup_in_progress, true);
677   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread was initialized, proceeding with cleanup");
678 
679   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Setting initialized to false");
680   g_debug_state_request.initialized = false; // Prevent double-join
681 
682   // Signal the thread to wake up immediately instead of waiting for 100ms timeout
683   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Signaling thread to exit");
684   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_exit, true);
685   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
686   log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Signal sent, about to join thread");
687 
688   // Use a timeout join to ensure we don't deadlock, but still unregister the thread
689   // The debug thread should exit quickly after should_exit is set above
690   int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_debug_thread, NULL, 1000000000ULL); // 1 second timeout
691   if (join_result == 0) {
692     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread joined successfully");
693   } else if (join_result == -2) {
694     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread join timed out (thread may still be running)");
695     // Don't unregister if timeout - thread is still alive
696   } else {
697     log_debug("[DEBUG_SYNC_CLEANUP] Thread join failed with error %d", join_result);
698   }
699 }






References atomic_store_bool().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy(),
    debug_sync_destroy(), and session_client_like_run().

int
  debug_sync_cond_timedwait (cond_t * cond, mutex_t * mutex, uint64_t
  timeout_ns, const char * file_name, int line_number, const char *
  function_name)


Definition at line 786 of file sync.c.


787                                                          {
788   // Same as debug_sync_cond_wait(): skip tracking the atomic unlock/relock.
789   (void)file_name;
790   (void)line_number;
791   (void)function_name;
792   return cond_timedwait_impl(cond, mutex, timeout_ns);
793 }






References mutex.






int
  debug_sync_cond_wait (cond_t * cond, mutex_t * mutex, const char * file_name,
  int line_number, const char * function_name)


Definition at line 775 of file sync.c.


776                                                     {
777   // Note: pthread_cond_wait() atomically releases and re-acquires the mutex,
778   // but this happens at the kernel level. Debug tracking can't monitor this atomic
779   // operation properly, so we skip cond_on_wait() to avoid false deadlock reports.
780   (void)file_name;
781   (void)line_number;
782   (void)function_name;
783   return cond_wait_impl(cond, mutex);
784 }






References mutex.

void
  debug_sync_print_backtrace_delayed (uint64_t delay_ns)


Schedule delayed backtrace printing on debug thread.


Parameters


delay_ns Nanoseconds to sleep before
  printing


Definition at line 608 of file sync.c.


608                                                            {
609   mutex_lock(&g_debug_state_request.mutex);
610   g_debug_state_request.request_type = DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE;
611   g_debug_state_request.delay_ns = delay_ns;
612   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_run, true);
613   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
614   mutex_unlock(&g_debug_state_request.mutex);
615 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    DEBUG_REQUEST_BACKTRACE.






void
  debug_sync_print_state (void )


Definition at line 378 of file sync.c.


378                                   {
379 // Use a single large buffer for all sync state output
380 #define SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE 65536  // Increased from 8192 to handle many syncs
381   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] ENTRY");
382 
383   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE, char *);
384   if (!buffer) {
385     log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Failed to allocate buffer");
386     return;
387   }
388 
389   sync_buffer_t buf = {.buffer = buffer, .buffer_size = SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE, .offset = 0};
390 
391   // Iterate through all registered syncs
392   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating mutexes");
393   named_registry_for_each(mutex_iter_callback, &buf);
394   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating rwlocks");
395   named_registry_for_each(rwlock_iter_callback, &buf);
396   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Iterating conds");
397   named_registry_for_each(cond_iter_callback, &buf);
398 
399   named_registry_for_each(atomic_t_iter_callback, &buf);
400   named_registry_for_each(atomic_ptr_iter_callback, &buf);
401 
402   // Print lock stacks for deadlock analysis
403   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Getting lock stacks");
404   debug_sync_print_lock_stacks(buf.buffer, buf.buffer_size, &buf.offset);
405 
406   // Log everything in one call
407   log_debug("[debug_sync_print_state] Buffer size: %zu bytes", buf.offset);
408   if (buf.offset > 0) {
409     log_info("SYNC_STATE:\n%s", buf.buffer);
410   } else {
411     log_info("SYNC_STATE: (empty)");
412   }
413 
414   SAFE_FREE(buffer);
415 #undef SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE
416 }






References sync_buffer_t::buffer,
    sync_buffer_t::buffer_size, named_registry_for_each(),
    sync_buffer_t::offset, and SYNC_BUFFER_SIZE.


Referenced by crypto_handshake_init().

void
  debug_sync_set_memory_report_interval (uint64_t interval_ns)


Set periodic memory report interval.

interval_ns Interval in nanoseconds (0 to
  disable)

int
  debug_sync_start_thread (void )


Definition at line 648 of file sync.c.


648                                   {
649   // Initialize mutex and condition variable for signal wakeup
650   if (!g_debug_state_request.initialized) {
651     mutex_init(&g_debug_state_request.mutex, "debug_sync_state");
652     cond_init(&g_debug_state_request.cond, "debug_sync_signal");
653     g_debug_state_request.initialized = true;
654   }
655 
656   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.should_exit, false);
657   int err = asciichat_thread_create(&g_debug_thread, "debug_sync", debug_print_thread_fn, NULL);
658   return err;
659 }






References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), and mutex_init().


Referenced by main().






void
  debug_sync_trigger_print (void )


Definition at line 701 of file sync.c.


701                                     {
702   // Set flag to trigger printing on debug thread (from SIGUSR1 handler).
703   // We don't call debug_sync_print_state() directly here to avoid logging
704   // in signal handler context, which could deadlock with logging mutexes.
705   // Uses atomic_store for thread-safe flag setting from signal handler.
706   //
707   // Signal the condition variable to wake up the debug thread immediately
708   // (without waiting for the 100ms timeout).
709   atomic_store_bool(&g_debug_state_request.signal_triggered, true);
710   cond_signal(&g_debug_state_request.cond);
711 }






References atomic_store_bool().


Referenced by session_handle_keyboard_input().
